name: duplicate-subject-two-dispatched
description: #47 DR-10: the fold-tier EXPOSURE of two records that share a subject. Two `dispatched` records for the same subject (an in-flight duplicate that slipped past, or a post-return re-dispatch) resolve to ONE dispatch entry - highest precedence then latest-`at` wins (both dispatched, so latest-`at`), and the earlier dispatched is EXPOSED in `superseded`, never merged or dropped (Detect-Dispatches.ps1:200-215). This is the fold-tier half of the duplicate-mint story: the producer's stateful refusal guard (design DR-9, proven in scripts/tests/Producer.Tests.ps1) stops in-flight duplicates at the mint; anything that reaches the store anyway is surfaced HERE, so both are visible (Law 4), keep-latest with the earlier disclosed. Provenance: OBSERVED - expected block generated by running scripts/Detect-Dispatches.ps1 on this exact input (2026-07-24, now=2026-07-22T10:05:00Z), reviewed against the precedence/supersession logic at Detect-Dispatches.ps1:190-215 and the sibling precedence-dispatched-then-returned.json.
